sourceMovie.title: Strange Days
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Strange Days for the same reason it broke through in 1995: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Kathryn Bigelow keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Chinatown
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Strange Days clicked for you, Chinatown (1974) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Strange Days?
faq.answer: Try Chinatown, City of Lies, Dead Again—each shares DNA with Strange Days while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Strange Days?
faq.answer: Strange Days (1995) is best described as Crime, Drama, Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Strange Days worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
